memoryArtifacts.headline: Capt. Anthony Eames (9 January 1595 – 6 October 1686)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Capt. Anthony Eames was born about 1595 in Fordington, Dorsetshire, England.1,2 He was the son of Thomas Eames and Millicent Brewster.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Biographical Info
memoryArtifacts.description: Anthony14 Eames {[Captain]} was born in Fordington, Dorsetshire, England 1595. He came to America aboard the "Recovery Of London" on 31 March 1633/34. While he lived at Fordington, he served as Church …

memoryArtifacts.headline: Anthony Eames, aChurchwarden, a loyal leader, and a Devoted Father
memoryArtifacts.description: Anthony Eames was born and raised in Fordington England. He was elected to be a churchwarden by the vicar or congregation. As a churchwarden his duty was to handle the secular and legal affairs of the …

memoryArtifacts.headline: Eames, Anthony
memoryArtifacts.description:
ANTHONY EAMES
Anthony Eames, born in England probably about 1590.  Was at Charlestown with wife Margery in 1634.  Removed to Hingham in 1637 where he was deputy for several years.  He was first milita …

zh.lifeSummary: 當John Eames於1586年出生在英國多瑟特福丁頓時，他的父親Thomas Eames38歲，母親Millicent35歲。他於大約1617年與Lucy Seager在英國英格蘭多瑟特福丁頓結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1662年在英國多瑟特多爾斯特享年76歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭多瑟特福丁頓。

nameMeanings.name: Eames
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English eme, eim ‘uncle’ (Old English ēam). Most of the modern variants of the surname have post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When John Eames was born in 1586, in Fordington, Dorset, England, his father, Thomas Eames, was 38 and his mother, Millicent, was 35. He married Lucy Seager about 1617, in Fordington, Dorset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1662, in Dorchester, Dorset, England, at the age of 76, and was buried in Fordington, Dorset, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Eames est né en 1586, à Fordington, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Eames, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Millicent, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Lucy Seager environ 1617, à Fordington, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1662, à Dorchester, Dorset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterré à Fordington, Dorset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Eames è nato il nel 1586 nel Fordington, Dorset, England, il suo padre, Thomas Eames, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Millicent, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Lucy Seager circa 1617, nel Fordington, Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1662, nel Dorchester, Dorset, England, all'età di 76 anni, e fu sepolto a Fordington, Dorset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: John Eames(이)가 1586년 영국 도르세트 포딩턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Eames(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Millicent(은)는 35세였다. 그는 대략 1617년 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 포딩턴에서 Lucy Seager(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1662년 영국 도르세트 도르체스터에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 도르세트 포딩턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Eames nasceu em 1586, em Fordington, Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Eames, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Millicent, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Lucy Seager aproximadamente 1617, em Fordington, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1662, em Dorchester, Dorset, Inglaterra, com 76 anos, e foi sepultado em Fordington, Dorset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Eames родился в 1586 г. в местности фордингтон, дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Eames было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Millicent, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Lucy Seager около 1617 г. в местности фордингтон, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1662 г. в местности дорчестир, дорсет, Англия в возрасте 76 лет и был похоронен в местности фордингтон, дорсет, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Eames nació en 1586, en Fordington, Dorset, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Eames, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Millicent, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Lucy Seager aproximadamente 1617, en Fordington, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1662, en Dorchester, Dorset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultado en Fordington, Dorset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
